summary: Matched several small functions in src/melee/ty/tyfigupon.c and promoted their symbols from generic un_/fn_ names to tyFigupon_ names. The PR added typed C implementations for tyFigupon_80314AA8, tyFigupon_80314BE4, and tyFigupon_80316BF8, renamed un_80314C5C to tyFigupon_80314C5C, updated callsites in tyfigupon.s and config/GALE01/symbols.txt, and refined struct un_804D6EF4_t to expose an HSD_Archive* archive field at offset 0x50 used by animation asset lookups.
what_changed: Implemented tyFigupon_80314AA8 in C. It conditionally resolves animation, material animation, and shape animation public addresses from un_804D6EF4->archive, calls HSD_JObjAddAnimAll, then requests frame 0.0f with HSD_JObjReqAnimAll.

Implemented tyFigupon_80314BE4 in C. It sets the current camera object from a gobj, clears the screen with erase color RGBA 0x19/0x19/0x33/0xFF, runs HSD_GObj_80390ED0(gobj, 7), disables fog via HSD_FogSet(NULL), and ends the current camera.
Renamed and retained the existing C function body for un_80314C5C as tyFigupon_80314C5C, with assembly references updated to call the renamed symbol.
Implemented tyFigupon_80316BF8 in C. It plays or triggers audio id 0x35 through lbAudioAx_80023F28 and removes or manipulates HSD_GObj_804D7838 via HSD_GObjProc_8038FE24.
Updated tyfigupon.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholders to typed signatures for the newly matched tyFigupon functions.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to rename un_80314AA8, fn_80314BE4, un_80314C5C, and fn_80316BF8 to tyFigupon_* symbols.
Updated asm/melee/ty/tyfigupon.s labels and branch targets so remaining assembly references use tyFigupon_80314AA8 and tyFigupon_80314C5C.
Refined struct un_804D6EF4_t in src/melee/un/types.h by shrinking pad_1C and adding an HSD_Archive* archive field at offset 0x50.
smart_moves: Exposed the archive pointer in un_804D6EF4_t instead of repeatedly treating the global structure as opaque padding; this made tyFigupon_80314AA8 readable and typed.
Preserved address-based suffixes while adding the module prefix tyFigupon_, keeping symbols searchable and stable for decomp progress tracking.
Matched a reusable helper, tyFigupon_80314AA8, that centralizes the repeated pattern of resolving optional animation resources and applying them to a JObj.
Kept null checks for each animation string before HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress, matching the assembly behavior and documenting that each animation channel is optional.
Added the specific baselib includes needed for CObj, archive, display, fog, GObj proc, and JObj APIs rather than relying only on placeholder declarations.
Updated symbols.txt, header declarations, C symbols, and remaining assembly callsites together, avoiding stale cross-file symbol references.
decomp_lessons: When a helper repeatedly loads resources from a global archive-like structure, exposing the exact pointer field in the shared struct can unlock clean typed matches.
For animation helpers, model absent resource names as NULL-resolved joints and still call HSD_JObjAddAnimAll with nullable anim/matanim/shapeanim pointers.
Small display/camera callbacks can often be matched directly by following the HSD_CObjSetCurrent/HSD_SetEraseColor/HSD_CObjEraseScreen/HSD_CObjEndCurrent sequence.
After renaming a function with remaining assembly callers, update both the .s label and all branch/callback address references in the assembly slice.
Replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with concrete signatures improves later callsite matching and reduces type ambiguity across the module.
Symbol table changes should accompany function matches so the build and decomp tooling report the new module-qualified names consistently.
naming_conventions: Use the module prefix tyFigupon_ for functions in src/melee/ty/tyfigupon.c once they are understood or matched enough to leave generic un_/fn_ naming.
Retain the function address suffix in names such as tyFigupon_80314AA8, tyFigupon_80314BE4, tyFigupon_80314C5C, and tyFigupon_80316BF8 when no semantic name is established.
Use typed parameter names that reflect resource roles, such as anim_str, matanim_str, and shapeanim_str for archive public-address lookups.
Use HSD-prefixed baselib type names in public prototypes, e.g. HSD_JObj*, HSD_GObj*, and HSD_Archive*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(16) in tyFigupon_80314AA8, indicating stack padding was needed to preserve the original frame layout for matching.
Matched optional archive lookup branches by assigning local HSD_AnimJoint*, HSD_MatAnimJoint*, and HSD_ShapeAnimJoint* variables to either HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress(...) results or NULL.
Used the known 0.0f argument to HSD_JObjReqAnimAll after HSD_JObjAddAnimAll to reproduce the animation initialization sequence.
Matched the CObj callback by preserving the conditional around HSD_CObjSetCurrent(GET_COBJ(gobj)) and keeping all camera/erase/fog calls inside it.
Updated assembly branch targets from un_80314AA8 to tyFigupon_80314AA8 and callback address loads from un_80314C5C to tyFigupon_80314C5C without changing instruction bytes.
Converted a tiny assembly stub at 0x80316BF8 into simple C calls to lbAudioAx_80023F28(0x35) and HSD_GObjProc_8038FE24(HSD_GObj_804D7838).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace tyFigupon_80314AA8 with a semantic name or reuse its animation-loading helper pattern.
Search for other users of struct un_804D6EF4_t offset 0x50 to validate the HSD_Archive* archive field across the un/ty modules.
Look for later tyfigupon PRs that match un_80314B54, fn_803152BC, un_803153EC, or fn_80316C24.
Search for similar HSD_CObjSetCurrent/HSD_SetEraseColor/HSD_CObjEraseScreen callbacks in other modules to reuse the tyFigupon_80314BE4 matching pattern.
